---
title: "Paul A. Kindermann"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: personalphoto.jpeg
  image-shape: rectangle
  image-width: 16rem
  links:
    - text: Email
      icon: envelope-fill
      href: mailto:paul.kindermann@soton.ac.uk
    - text: Bluesky
      icon: bluesky
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/paul-kindermann.net
---

I am a postdoc at the [University of Southampton](https://www.southampton.ac.uk/people/66znvm/doctor-paul-kindermann), Department of Politics, and a visiting fellow at the LSE [European Institute](https://www.lse.ac.uk/european-institute/people/kindermann-paul), working at the intersection of political sociology and normative political theory. In my [research](research.qmd), I specialise in the comparative study of political participation and institutional design in Europe, and I have a strong interest in the European Union; my [first article](https://doi.org/10.1080/13501763.2025.2554911) on the role of democratic innovations in the EU has been published in the Journal of European Public Policy. See [my CV](CV.qmd) for further details on my background.

During my graduate studies in Europe and the United States, I have taught seminars on these subjects at LSE, in Frankfurt, and Hamburg. Besides, I am a mentor at the Friedrich-Ebert-Foundation. Please [get in touch](contact.qmd) about collaborations, ideas, and questions!

<!-- ## Research -->

<!-- Simulating democratic reform in the EU: self-legitimation through participatory innovation, *Journal of European Public Policy*, 2025, DOI: [10.1080/13501763.2025.2554911](https://doi.org/10.1080/13501763.2025.2554911) -->

<!-- Politicising democratic innovations? Evidence on the divided public resonance of minipublics, Working paper, with [Elena Pro](https://elena-pr.github.io) -->
